content.Text:
[Mon 2026-04-20 17:09 PDT] Create a new interactive HTML tool at docs/graph-merge.html for GraphVisual - a "Graph Merge Workshop" that lets users combine two graphs using set operations (Union, Intersection, Difference, Symmetric Difference) with animated visualization. IMPORTANT: Look at existing HTML files in docs/ (e.g., graph-compare.html, resilience.html) for the project's style conventions. Use the same dark theme (link styles.css), sidebar nav pattern, Canvas-based graph rendering, and button styles. ## Feature Requirements: 1. **Two Graph Editors** (side-by-side panels): - Each panel has a Canvas for drawing nodes (click to add) and edges (click two nodes) - Adjacency list textarea for text input - 6 presets each: Triangle, Square, Star K5, Path P5, Cycle C6, Petersen - Clear button, random graph button - Nodes are draggable 2. **Four Set Operations** (big buttons between/below panels): - **Union (G₁ ∪ G₂)**: All nodes and edges from both - **Intersection (G₁ ∩ G₂)**: Only nodes/edges in both - **Difference (G₁ \ G₂)**: Nodes/edges in G₁ but not G₂ - **Symmetric Difference (G₁ △ G₂)**: Edges in exactly one graph 3. **Result Panel** (below): - Large Canvas showing the merged graph with force-directed layout - Color-coded: nodes/edges from G₁ only = blue, G₂ only = green, both = purple - Animation: nodes fade in with a staggered entrance - Stats: node count, edge count, components, density 4. **Proactive Insights** (agentic): - After each operation, automatically detect and display: - "The union created X new connections between previously separate components" - "The intersection preserved the core structure: a K3 subgraph" - "Symmetric difference reveals X edges unique to each graph" - Suggest the next most interesting operation to try - Warn if result is empty or trivial 5. **Export**: JSON adjacency list of result, copy to clipboard ## Style: - Use the exact same CSS variables and classes from styles.css - Dark theme (#0d1117 background) - Sidebar with logo linking to index.html, and a link to this page marked active - Canvas with var(--bg) background, var(--border) borders - Buttons using .btn / .btn-primary / .btn-secondary patterns from other pages - Keep ALL code in a single HTML file (inline styles + inline script), linking only styles.css ## Sidebar nav should include: ```html
📊

GraphVisual

Documentation
 🏠Home 🔀Merge 📈Statistics
``` Do NOT modify any existing files. Only create docs/graph-merge.html.
